Test Description: Default value check and masked write/read verification across GPIO registers using arrays from test_define.c; executes two phases: reset value check and write/read check.
Remarks:
- Some registers are intentionally skipped via skip_array and skip_rst_array.
- Default value compare masks LSB with 0xfffffffe.
- Comment note: when reading default values, DIN may become 1 if not forced; forcing 0 may set level select high, affecting expected read.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry: test_case()
1. Call chk_rst_val().
   1.1 Loop i = 0..CNT-1 (CNT=49): addr = addr_array[i].
       1.1.1 If (skip_rst_array[i] == 1) -> continue (skip this address).
       1.1.2 If (read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) -> continue (not readable).
       1.1.3 READ: data_rd = read_reg(addr).
       1.1.4 MODIFY: data = (data_rd & 0xfffffffe) // mask LSB.
       1.1.5 COMPARE: if (data == default_value_array[i]) -> pass branch; else { def_fail_cnt++; printf failure }.
2. Call chk_rd_wr().
   2.1 Define chk_val[6] = {0xffffffff, 0xaaaaaaaa, 0x55555555, 0xf5f5f5f5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xffff0000}.
   2.2 For each pattern j = 0..5: data_wr = chk_val[j].
       2.2.1 WRITE phase (i = 0..CNT-1): addr = addr_array[i].
             a) If (skip_array[i] == 1) -> continue.
             b) If (write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) -> continue.
             c) WRITE: write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i])).
       2.2.2 READ/COMPARE phase (i = 0..CNT-1): addr = addr_array[i].
             a) If (skip_array[i] == 1) -> continue.
             b) If (write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) -> continue.
             c) If (read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) -> continue.
             d) READ+MASK: data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]).
             e) COMPUTE: wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xffffffff).
             f) COMPUTE exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i]) ).
             g) COMPARE: if (data_rd == exp_val) -> pass; else { wr_fail_cnt++; printf mismatch }.
3. Finalize: if (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0) finish(1); else finish(0).
Impacted Registers: MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_9, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_10, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_11, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_12, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_13, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_14, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_15, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_16, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_17, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_18, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_19, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_20, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_21, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_22, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_23, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_24, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_25, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_26, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_27, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_28, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_29, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_30, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_31, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_32, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_33, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_34, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_35, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_36, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_37, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_38, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_39, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR2_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR2_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DOUT_GROUP1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DOUT_GROUP2, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DOUT_GROUP3, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DOUT_GROUP4, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DIN_GROUP1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DIN_GROUP2, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DIN_GROUP3, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DIN_GROUP4
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- chk_rst_val(): PASS if (read_reg(addr) & 0xfffffffe) == default_value_array[i]; else def_fail_cnt++ and prints failure.
- chk_rd_wr(): PASS if for each eligible address, (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]) == exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | ((~write_mask_array[i]) & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])); else wr_fail_cnt++ and prints mismatch.
- Final decision: finish(0) if (def_fail_cnt == 0 && wr_fail_cnt == 0); else finish(1).

Test Description: Enable negedge interrupt per GPIO[8..39], generate falling edges sequentially, validate per-pin DIN=0, raw/group status set then cleared, and sysreg routing; bounded waits with timeout.
Remarks:
- int_pend used for ISR synchronization; bounded wait with timeout=5000 and wait_on(10).
- Edge generation via pad driver at 0xA0243ffc; arm int_pend before edge to avoid race.
- Per-pin raw and group status are cleared in ISR.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry: test_case()
1. test_err = 0.
2. Conditional IRQ enables:
   2.1 Ifdef GPIO0: GIC_EnableIRQ(87).
   2.2 Ifdef GPIO1: GIC_EnableIRQ(88).
3. Conditional sysreg interrupt enable:
   3.1 Ifdef GPIO0: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 = LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR.
   3.2 Ifdef GPIO1: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 = LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR.
4. WRITE: 0xA0243ffc = 0xffffffff (drive all high initially).
5. Configure GPIOs 8..39 for input + negedge + clear raw:
   Loop i = 0..31:
   5.1 addr1 = (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4)).
   5.2 WRITE: (1u<<20)|(1u<<18)|(1u<<16) // doe=1, neie=1, iclr=1.
   5.3 wait_on(10).
6. For each pin i, enable, generate falling edge, and wait with timeout:
   Loop i = 0..31:
   6.1 wr_val = (1u<<i).
   6.2 WRITE: MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1 = wr_val (pre-clear group raw).
   6.3 WRITE: MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1 = wr_val (enable only this bit).
   6.4 wait_on(10).
   6.5 int_pend = 1 (arm BEFORE edge).
   6.6 WRITE: 0xA0243ffc = 0xffffffff; wait_on(30).
   6.7 WRITE: 0xA0243ffc = ~wr_val (create falling edge on bit i).
   6.8 TIMEOUT loop: timeout=5000; while (int_pend && timeout--) wait_on(10).
   6.9 If (timeout == 0) { printf timeout; test_err++; }.
7. finish(test_err).

ISR: Default_IRQHandler()
8. local_wr = (1u<<i); int_pend = 0.
9. WRITE: 0xA0243ffc = 0xffffffff (return to known high state).
10. raddr = (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4)); READ: rdata = read_reg(raddr).
11. If ((rdata & 0x1) != 0) { test_err++; } // DIN must be 0 for negedge.
12. If ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0) {
    12.1 READ: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1).
    12.2 If ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) { test_err++; } // group bit must be set.
    12.3 raddr2 = (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4)); WRITE: (1u<<20)|(1u<<16) // doe=1, iclr=1.
    12.4 WRITE: MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1 = local_wr.
    12.5 READ: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); If (rdata_grp != 0x0) { test_err++; } // group clear verify.
    12.6 Ifdef GPIO0: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 = LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR; GIC_ClearIRQ(87).
         Ifdef GPIO1: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 = LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR; GIC_ClearIRQ(88).
   } else { test_err++; }
Impacted Registers: MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Timeout in main loop: PASS if int_pend cleared before timeout; FAIL if timeout==0 (printf + test_err++).
- DIN check (ISR): PASS if ((rdata & 0x1) == 0); FAIL otherwise.
- Raw/group status (ISR): PASS if ((rdata & 0x2) != 0) AND ((rdata_grp & local_wr) != 0); FAIL otherwise.
- Group clear (ISR): PASS if post-clear read of MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 returns 0x0; FAIL otherwise.
- Sysreg clear: PASS if write to MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 followed by IRQ clear removes status; any mismatch increments test_err.
- Final result: finish(test_err) where PASS is test_err==0.

Test Description: Enable posedge interrupt for GPIO[8..39], route via sysreg, generate rising edges sequentially, validate group interrupt observed and cleared, and sysreg status cleared.
Remarks:
- volatile int_pend used for ISR synchronization; bounded wait with timeout=2000 and wait_on(10).
- Group interrupt masked during service and re-enabled after clear.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry: test_case()
1. Conditional IRQ enables:
   1.1 Ifdef GPIO0: GIC_EnableIRQ(87).
   1.2 Ifdef GPIO1: GIC_EnableIRQ(88).
2. test_err = 0; declare locals.
3. Conditional sysreg interrupt enable:
   3.1 Ifdef GPIO0: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 = LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR.
   3.2 Ifdef GPIO1: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 = LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR.
4. Configure posedge per pin: Loop i=0..31: WRITE (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) = 0x00020000.
5. wait_on(10).
6. Set GPIO 8..39 to input (doe=1 per group):
   WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1 = 0x000000FF.
   WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2 = 0x000000FF.
   WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3 = 0x000000FF.
   WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4 = 0x000000FF.
7. wait_on(10).
8. Enable group interrupt output: WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1 = 0xFFFFFFFF.
9. For each pin i generate rising edge and wait with timeout:
   9.1 WRITE: 0xA0243ffc = 0x00000000 (ensure low), wait_on(10).
   9.2 int_pend = 1 (arm BEFORE edge).
   9.3 WRITE: 0xA0243ffc = 0xFFFFFFFF (rising edge).
   9.4 TIMEOUT loop: timeout=2000; while ((int_pend==1) && (--timeout>0)) wait_on(10).
   9.5 If (timeout==0) { printf timeout; test_err++; break; }.
   9.6 WRITE: 0xA0243ffc = 0x00000000; wait_on(10).
10. finish(test_err).

ISR: Default_IRQHandler()
11. wr_val = (1<<i); int_pend = 0.
12. READ: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1).
13. WRITE: MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1 = 0x00000000 (mask during service).
14. If ((rdata_grp & 0xffffffff) != 0) { // success } else { printf error; test_err++; }.
15. Clear per-pin raw: Loop j=0..31: WRITE (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + j*4) = 0x00010000; wait_on(2).
16. READ: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); If (rdata_grp == 0x0) { success } else { printf error; test_err++; }.
17. Sysreg clear and verify:
    17.1 Ifdef GPIO0: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 = LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR; READ rdata; If ((rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR) != 0) { printf error; test_err++; }.
    17.2 Ifdef GPIO1: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 = LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR; READ rdata; If ((rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR) != 0) { printf error; test_err++; }.
18. WRITE: MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1 = 0xFFFFFFFF (re-enable).
19. Ifdef GPIO0: GIC_ClearIRQ(87); Ifdef GPIO1: GIC_ClearIRQ(88).
Impacted Registers: MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Group interrupt presence: PASS if (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & 0xffffffff) != 0; FAIL otherwise.
- Group interrupt clear: PASS if post-clear read of MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 == 0x0; FAIL otherwise.
- Sysreg status clear: PASS if post-clear read of MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 has the relevant bit cleared; FAIL otherwise.
- Timeout handling: PASS if int_pend cleared before timeout; FAIL if timeout==0.
- Final result: finish(test_err) where PASS is test_err==0.
